pr.title: Fix itthunder by removing unnecessary constants, mark as matching
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: PR 2210 made src/melee/it/items/itthunder.c matching by removing unnecessary global float constants from the itthunder header, replacing use of the zero constant with a direct 0.0f literal, relabeling two sdata2 entries as local symbols, and flipping itthunder.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py.
what_changed: Changed config/GALE01/symbols.txt so .sdata2 symbols at 0x804DD3A8 and 0x804DD3AC are local anonymous labels @149 and @175 instead of global it_804DD3A8 and it_804DD3AC.
Changed configure.py to mark melee/it/items/itthunder.c as Object(Matching) instead of Object(NonMatching).
In it_802CCC68, replaced assignment item->xDD4_itemVar.thunder.x68 = it_804DD3A8 with item->xDD4_itemVar.thunder.x68 = 0.0f.
Removed header-level definitions f32 it_804DD3AC = 1.0f and f32 it_804DD3A8 = 0.0f from src/melee/it/items/itthunder.h.
smart_moves: Avoided exporting simple float constants from the item header when the code could match with a literal 0.0f.
Cleaned up the symbol map to reflect that the float constants did not need named global symbols for this match.
Kept the code change minimal: one C assignment, two symbol-label changes, header cleanup, and the build status flip to Matching.
decomp_lessons: Do not introduce or keep global float constants in headers just to satisfy a decomp attempt when a direct literal produces the correct match.
If a constant is not semantically useful or externally referenced, prefer local sdata2 labels over named global symbols in symbols.txt.
Header files should declare interfaces and extern data where needed, not define ad-hoc data objects such as f32 constants.
Once a file is confirmed matching, update configure.py from NonMatching to Matching so the build system tracks the current decomp status.
naming_conventions: Global item state table symbol retained as it_803F7C40.
Unnecessary global constant names it_804DD3A8 and it_804DD3AC were removed in favor of local labels @149 and @175.
Item function names in the slice remain address-style, e.g. it_802CCC68 and itThunder_UnkMotion0_Coll.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use direct float literals such as 0.0f where they match, rather than forcing references to named .sdata2 globals.
Relabel unused or non-semantic sdata2 float entries as local symbols to avoid misleading global symbol exports.
A small constant-use correction can be enough to move an item source file from NonMatching to Matching.
follow_up_queries: Find other item headers defining f32 constants directly instead of using literals or extern declarations.
Search symbols.txt for named global .sdata2 float constants that may only be local compiler-generated constants.
Check remaining NonMatching item files for unnecessary global constant references blocking matching.
